key_insight: A digital-learning, social-business model can expand access to technical and vocational education for grassroots students by delivering free, understandable vocational content online and pairing it with open-book assessment.
engineering_relevance: For welding and other shop-floor disciplines, the approach can improve workforce readiness by combining scalable theory delivery with offline practical training and certification partnerships.
method: Publish vocational theory lessons on YouTube in simple language
Enhance lessons with interactive 3D models and live sessions
Use open-book online exams plus offline institution-based practical training
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a proposed model rather than experimental results, so effectiveness is framed by intended accessibility, flexibility, and employability outcomes rather than measured data.
watch_item: Access, device availability, and the need to preserve hands-on skill quality may limit impact if online theory is not matched by sufficient practical training.
paper_title: Making Technical & Vocational Education Accessible to the Grassroots Students through Digital Learning
